STEP 1: IDENTITY
-----------------
Name: Aashna Avachat
Organization: Neighborhood Literary Agency
Role: Literary Agent
Pronouns: she/her
Email: aashna@neighborhoodlit.com
Submission portal: QueryTracker
Availability: OPEN

STEP 2: GLOBAL CONDITIONS
--------------------------
- authors/illustrators of color only → REQUIRED
- own-voices stories with protagonists of color → STRONGLY PREFERRED
- queer representation, religious diversity, disability representation → PREFERRED

STEP 3: PREFERENCE SECTIONS
-----------------------------
[Picture Books]
  Audience: picture_books
  Genres: contemporary, family, SEL
  Wants:
    - Laugh-out-loud funny with clever illustration potential
    - Themes: family, culture, SEL, nature/environment
  Does Not Want:
    - Food-based books
    - Non-fiction picture books
    - Biographies
  Conditions:
    - Fiction only

[Young Adult]
  Audience: young_adult, new_adult
  Genres: romance, mystery, thriller, dark academia, cozy fantasy
  Wants:
    - Romantic comedies where both leads are POC
    - Plot-driven horror
    - Dark academia with political critique of academia
  Tropes Wanted:
    - forced proximity
    - enemies to lovers
  Tropes Excluded:
    - instalove
  Comp Titles:
    - I'd Tell You I Love You by Ally Carter — spy/heist YA tone

[Adult]
  Audience: adult
  Genres: literary fiction, mystery, horror
  Wants:
    - Funny contemporary fiction
    - Domestic thrillers
  Does Not Want:
    - Police-focused mysteries

STEP 4: HARD NOS (Global)
--------------------------
Content nos:
  - sexual violence on the page
  - child abuse
  - war novels
Format nos:
  - poetry collections
  - short story collections
  - board books
  - epistolary formats
Trope nos:
  - non-consensual romance
  - teacher x student
Category nos:
  - children's non-fiction

STEP 5: SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS
---------------------------------
Picture Books:
  Submit via: QueryTracker
  Materials: query letter, full PB text in writing sample field
  Special notes: include dummy/art samples if applicable
Young Adult:
  Submit via: QueryTracker
  Materials: query letter, synopsis, first 10 pages
- Unsolicited queries via email are deleted unread

STEP 6: COMP TITLES & TASTE REFERENCES
----------------------------------------
A) HIGH-PRIORITY COMPS
  - The Clique by Lisi Harrison — but with protagonists of color
  - Yellowjackets — YA wilderness camp horror tone

B) TASTE REFERENCES (personal favorites — not submission comps)
  Books: Pachinko by Min Jin Lee, Severance by Ling Ma
  Film/TV: Yellowjackets, Fleabag
  Music: Mitski

STEP 7: CROSS-CUTTING THEMES
------------------------------
- found family
- queer joy
- morally grey characters
- humor that makes you laugh AND cry

STEP 8: CONFIDENCE FLAGS
--------------------------
INFERRED — country (US, inferred from agency)
INFERRED — is_open (active MSWL page, no closed statement)
NUANCED — fantasy is hard-no globally BUT cozy fantasy and light retellings explicitly wanted
MISSING — response time not stated
